entries.definition:
sh–ṭ–ṭ to be remote, far off, or beyond the acceptable limits; to act unjustly; side of a camel’s hump; bank or side of a river. Of this root, two forms occur three times in the Qurʾan: تُشْطِط tushṭiṭ once and شططًا shaṭaṭan twice.
تُشْطِط tushṭiṭ [imperf. v., intrans.] to go off the mark, to be far off, to digress, to exceed due bounds (38:22) فَاحْكُم بَيْنَنَا بِالْحَقِّ وَلَا تُشْطِطْ so judge between us fairly, and do not exceed the due bounds.

شططًا shaṭaṭan [v. n./n.] acting extravagantly or outrageously, deviation (18:14) لَّن نَّدْعُوَا مِن دُونِهِ إِلَٰهًا لَّقَدْ قُلْنَا إِذًا شَطَطًا We will never call upon any god other than Him, for then we would have uttered an outrageous thing.
entries.definition_english:

تُشْطِط tushṭiṭ [imperf. v., intrans.] to go off the mark, to be far off, to digress, to exceed due bounds (38:22) فَاحْكُم بَيْنَنَا بِالْحَقِّ وَلَا تُشْطِطْ so judge between us fairly, and do not exceed the due bounds.

شططًا shaṭaṭan [v. n./n.] acting extravagantly or outrageously, deviation (18:14) لَّن نَّدْعُوَا مِن دُونِهِ إِلَٰهًا لَّقَدْ قُلْنَا إِذًا شَطَطًا We will never call upon any god other than Him, for then we would have uttered an outrageous thing.
